project.name: Greenlit
project.assignment: Binge Labs AI Intern Take-Home (72hr deadline)
project.submission_angle: Built Content Approval Loop as primary problem. Extended infrastructure to also solve Performance Reporting (Problem 4) — two problems solved in one system.

problem_3_content_approval.title: Content Approval Loop
problem_3_content_approval.raw_description: After a script is written, it goes to the client for approval over WhatsApp or email. There is no tracking. Nobody knows which scripts are waiting for feedback, which have been approved, and which have been rejected. The team lead manually chases clients every 2 to 3 days. We have missed deadlines because a script was just sitting unseen in someone's inbox.

problem_4_performance_reporting.title: Performance Reporting
problem_4_performance_reporting.raw_description: At the end of each month, we pull content performance data from Instagram Insights and YouTube Studio manually, paste it into a Google Sheet, write a short summary, and email it to each client. This takes 2 to 3 hours per client. We have 22 active clients. We are always late with reports, and clients keep asking for them before we send them.

impact_metrics.problem: Always late, clients chasing reports before team sends them

tech_stack.frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion
tech_stack.hitl: OpenAI Agents SDK JS (RunState serialization for pause/resume)
tech_stack.whatsapp: Twilio WhatsApp Business API (sandbox for demo)
tech_stack.instagram_scraping: Apify Instagram Post Scraper API ($0.0027/result, ~2000 free results)
tech_stack.youtube_data: YouTube Data API v3 (official Google API, free, 10,000 units/day quota)
tech_stack.observability: LangSmith (free tier, 5000 traces/month, zero-config via env vars)

clients.notes: whatsapp_number added for Twilio integration. instagram_handle and youtube_channel_id added for performance reporting scraping.

scripts.notes: review_channel tracks whether client responded via email or WhatsApp — unified source of truth.

chasers.notes: critique_scores stores self-critique rubric results. node_execution_log stores each LangGraph node's execution summary for live trace UI.

client_memories.notes: Stores all client behavioral patterns as vector embeddings for RAG retrieval during chaser generation.

performance_reports.notes: NEW TABLE for performance reporting feature. Stores scraped data + Claude-generated summary.

whatsapp_messages.notes: NEW TABLE. Logs all WhatsApp interactions for audit trail and future RAG training.

functions.match_client_memories: RPC function using pgvector <=> cosine similarity. Returns top N memories for a given client and query embedding.
functions.signature: match_client_memories(query_embedding vector(1536), match_client_id uuid, match_count int DEFAULT 5)

agent_architecture.pattern: ReAct loop with self-critique — agent iterates until quality threshold met or max revisions reached

1_monitor.purpose: Query Supabase for scripts overdue >48hrs with no chaser sent in last 24hrs

2_ragRetrieval.purpose: Generate embedding for client context query. Call match_client_memories RPC. Returns top 5 semantic matches about client communication style, past approvals/rejections, tone preferences.
2_ragRetrieval.fallback: If no memories exist (new client), use recent scripts as context

3_sentimentAnalysis.purpose: NEW NODE. Analyze urgency level based on: hours overdue, client historical response time, deadline proximity. Output urgency score 1-10. Tone recommendation: formal/casual/urgent.

4_contextAssembly.purpose: Build ChatPromptTemplate combining: script details, hours overdue, client memory context, urgency score, tone recommendation.

5_generation.purpose: Call Claude claude-sonnet-4-5. Parse SUBJECT/BODY from response. Store chaser as pending_hitl in Supabase.

6_selfCritique.purpose: NEW NODE. Score draft against rubric using Claude as judge. Rubric: personalization (0-10), urgency clarity (0-10), tone match to client profile (0-10), call-to-action strength (0-10). If avgScore < 7: set needsRevision=true with critique notes.
6_selfCritique.output: { scores: {personalization, urgencyClarity, toneMatch, ctaStrength}, avgScore, needsRevision, critiqueNotes }

7_revision.purpose: NEW NODE. If needsRevision=true AND revisionCount < 2: call Claude again with original draft + critique notes. Output revised draft. Increment revisionCount.

8_memoryUpdate.purpose: After chaser approved and sent: embed the interaction as a new client memory. Stores: what tone was used, client response time, whether they approved/rejected/requested changes.

agent_architecture.graph_flow: START → ragRetrieval → sentimentAnalysis → contextAssembly → generation → selfCritique → [if needsRevision && revisionCount < 2] → revision → selfCritique (loop) → [if score >= 7 OR maxRevisions] → memoryUpdate → END

sse_streaming.mechanism: Each node pushes event to SSE stream: data: {node, status, summary, timestamp}\n\n
sse_streaming.storage: In-memory Map<scriptId, SSEWriter> for active streams (fine for demo)

whatsapp_integration.pricing: $0.005/message in production, free sandbox for demo
whatsapp_integration.sandbox_setup: Client texts 'join [sandbox-code]' to +1-415-523-8886 to opt into sandbox
whatsapp_integration.demo_approach: Use Twilio sandbox. For Loom: show outbound message sent, then simulate inbound reply via Twilio webhook test tool.

flow.step_1: Script created → POST /api/scripts triggers sendReviewEmail AND sendWhatsAppMessage
flow.step_2: Twilio sends WhatsApp to client.whatsapp_number: 'Hi [name], your script [title] is ready for review. Quick reply: APPROVE, REJECT, or type your feedback. Full review: [magic_link]'
flow.step_3: Client replies on WhatsApp → Twilio hits webhook POST /api/webhooks/whatsapp
flow.step_4: Webhook calls Claude to parse intent: approved / rejected / changes_requested + extract feedback text
flow.step_5: Update scripts table: status, client_feedback, review_channel='whatsapp', reviewed_at
flow.step_6: Store WhatsApp message in whatsapp_messages table for audit trail
flow.step_7: Supabase Realtime updates dashboard Kanban in real-time

whatsapp_integration.intent_parsing_prompt: You are parsing a WhatsApp reply from a content client. Reply: '[message]'. Classify intent as: APPROVED, REJECTED, CHANGES_REQUESTED, or UNCLEAR. If CHANGES_REQUESTED or REJECTED, extract the specific feedback. Return JSON: {intent, feedback, confidence}
whatsapp_integration.unified_inbox: Both email (magic link click) and WhatsApp (webhook parsed) write to same scripts.status field. Dashboard shows one unified view with review_channel badge showing how client responded.

instagram.approach: Apify Instagram Post Scraper API — input client's Instagram handle, get last 30 posts with likes, comments, views, engagement rate
instagram.free_tier: ~2000 results free on Apify free plan

youtube.approach: YouTube Data API v3 — official Google API, free, no scraping needed
youtube.quota: 10,000 units/day free. Channel stats = 1 unit. Video list = 1 unit per page. More than enough for 22 clients.
youtube.auth: YOUTUBE_API_KEY env var (Google Cloud Console, free)

report_generation_flow.step_1: Team clicks 'Generate Reports' button in /reports page
report_generation_flow.step_2: POST /api/reports/generate → loops through all active clients
report_generation_flow.step_3: For each client: fetch Instagram data via Apify + YouTube data via Google API in parallel (Promise.all)
report_generation_flow.step_4: Call Claude with: raw metrics + client's past reports (from performance_reports table) + client_memories (brand voice RAG) to generate narrative summary
report_generation_flow.step_5: Claude prompt: 'You are a social media analyst at Scrollhouse. Write a monthly performance report for [client_name]. Use their brand voice. Data: [metrics]. Past reports: [history]. Keep it under 200 words, highlight wins, explain drops, end with next month recommendation.'
report_generation_flow.step_6: Store in performance_reports table with status='draft'
report_generation_flow.step_7: Team reviews in /reports page — sees all client reports side by side
report_generation_flow.step_8: Click 'Send All' → Resend emails each report to respective client

ui_pages.reports_list: app/reports/page.tsx — grid of client cards showing: last report date, status (draft/sent), key metrics at a glance
ui_pages.report_detail: app/reports/[clientId]/page.tsx — full report view with Instagram + YouTube metrics visualized (recharts), Claude summary, edit textarea, Send button
ui_pages.generate_button: Dashboard header: 'Generate Reports' button (only visible to authenticated team)

live_agent_trace_ui.trigger: '▶ Run Agent' button in dashboard header (green, triggers most overdue script)

design.layout: Slide-in panel from right, dark overlay, z-50
design.node_pipeline: Horizontal nodes connected by animated lines: [Monitor] →→→ [RAG] →→→ [Sentiment] →→→ [Context] →→→ [Generate] →→→ [Critique] →→→ [Revise?] →→→ [Memory]
design.execution_log: Scrolling log below pipeline showing nodeExecutionLog entries as they stream in via SSE
design.critique_scores: When selfCritique node completes, show score bars: Personalization 8/10, Urgency Clarity 6/10, etc.

sse_implementation.headers: Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache, Connection: keep-alive
sse_implementation.event_format: data: {node: 'ragRetrieval', status: 'complete', summary: 'Retrieved 4 memories for Nike. Top: prefers formal tone (0.89 similarity)', timestamp: 1234567890}\n\n

client_intelligence_page.purpose: Makes RAG memory system visible and tangible to non-technical evaluator

pages.list: app/intelligence/page.tsx — grid of all clients with: response speed badge, approval rate bar, top behavioral insight
pages.detail: app/intelligence/[clientId]/page.tsx — full profile with AI-generated behavioral summary, all memories as chips, timeline of past scripts

ai_behavioral_profile.prompt: Based on these client interaction memories, write a 3-sentence behavioral profile for [client_name]: [memories]. Cover: 1) Communication style and preferences 2) What content they approve vs reject 3) Optimal outreach timing and tone.

client_intelligence_page.memory_chips: All client_memories displayed as color-coded chips: feedback=blue, approval=green, rejection=red, behavioral_pattern=purple

kpi_cards: Time saved: (chasers_sent × 15min) displayed as 'X hrs Y min'
charts: Line chart: Scripts sent per day last 30 days
Horizontal bar chart: Response time by client (slowest at top)

scripts_at_risk_table.definition: Scripts overdue AND client has avg_response_hours > 48
scripts_at_risk_table.risk_score: (hours_overdue / avg_response_hours) × 100, shown as red progress bar

hitl_timeline.design: Horizontal stepper below chaser email preview. Active stage has filled circle, future stages are outlined. Timestamps shown under each completed stage.

sequence.headline: fadeIn from y:20, scale from 0.95 to 1, delay: 0.15
sequence.divider: scaleX from 0 to 1, transformOrigin: center, delay: 0.45

steps: 3. Add all env vars in Vercel dashboard
4. Update GitHub OAuth app callback URL to https://greenlit.ruskaruma.me/api/auth/callback/github
deployment.seed_reset: GET /api/seed?reset=true — clears and re-seeds for clean demo state

items: Login page Framer Motion entrance animations
Live agent trace panel with SSE streaming
sentimentAnalysis node added to LangGraph agent
Update POST /api/scripts to trigger WhatsApp alongside email
Add review_channel field to scripts table
Dashboard: show channel badge (Email/WhatsApp) on script cards
Resend email integration for sending reports
Add instagram_handle + youtube_channel_id to clients table
Analytics tab with KPI cards + recharts
Smoke test all features on production URL
Submission writeup doc (problem reasoning + architecture + agent design + impact + what next)

sections.content: Open with the problem stat: '125 manual chases per month. Scripts sitting unseen. Missed deadlines. I built Greenlit to eliminate this entirely.'
Show beautiful login, GitHub OAuth flow, smooth animations
Show live Kanban. Point out: overdue cards with red glow, Live indicator, HITL badge count, theme toggle

sections.section: Script upload + dual channel send (1.5 min)
sections.content: Upload new script. Show email sent (Resend). Show WhatsApp message sent (Twilio). Explain: 'client can respond on either channel — both update the same system'
Simulate WhatsApp reply. Show Claude parsing intent. Dashboard updates in real time via Supabase Realtime.
Trigger agent manually for overdue script. Show AgentTracePanel: nodes lighting up one by one. Show critique scores. Show revision happening if score < 7. Narrate each node.
Go to /hitl. Show AI-generated chaser with HITL timeline. Edit it, approve it. Show email sends.
Go to /intelligence. Show Nike's behavioral profile. Show memories as chips. 'The agent knows Nike prefers formal tone because it learned from 6 past interactions.'
Go to /reports. Click 'Generate Reports'. Show Instagram + YouTube metrics loading. Show Claude-generated report in brand voice. One-click send vs 2.5 hours manually.
Switch to LangSmith tab. Show full agent trace: every node, every token, every decision. 'Full observability baked in from day one.'
Brief-to-Script pipeline using same LangGraph infrastructure. Same nodes, different context. Full content lifecycle in one system.

writeup_key_points.problem_selection_reasoning: Content Approval Loop has the highest blast radius: client relationships are the agency's only asset. A missed approval = a damaged relationship = churn. 125 manual chases/month = a full-time job that exists only because of missing infrastructure.
writeup_key_points.why_two_problems: While building the approval loop, the same Supabase + scraping + Claude infrastructure naturally solved Performance Reporting. Same client data model, same RAG memory, same email delivery. Showing this connection demonstrates systems thinking.
agent_design_decisions: TypeScript LangGraph over Python: single-repo Vercel deploy, no separate service to manage
Self-critique ReAct loop: agent evaluates own output quality before human review — reduces HITL load by catching bad drafts automatically
RAG over static prompts: agent learns from every client interaction. The more it runs, the better it gets.
HITL as escalation not default: agent only escalates when confidence is high enough that human time is worth spending
writeup_key_points.omnichannel_insight: Binge Labs clients communicate on WhatsApp. Building email-only would solve 40% of the problem. Twilio webhook + Claude intent parsing solves 100% — and the unification insight (single source of truth regardless of channel) is the real architectural win.
writeup_key_points.what_next: Brief-to-Script Pipeline (Problem 2): client submits brief via form → same LangGraph agent rewrites into internal format → assigns to scriptwriter → tracks status. Same infrastructure, 40-60 briefs/month automated. Full content lifecycle: Brief → Script → Approval → Report — all in Greenlit.

competitive_differentiation.vs_typical_submission: Most candidates build a form with a database. Greenlit has: stateful AI agent with self-critique loop, omnichannel client communication (Email + WhatsApp), RAG memory that improves over time, HITL with full audit trail, performance reporting with automated scraping, and LangSmith observability.

evaluation_criteria_coverage.problem_selection_20pct: Highest-damage problem + extends to solve Problem 4 as bonus
evaluation_criteria_coverage.agent_quality_30pct: LangGraph ReAct loop with self-critique, pgvector RAG, OpenAI Agents SDK HITL, LangSmith tracing
evaluation_criteria_coverage.architecture_tool_choices_20pct: Every tool justified, TypeScript for single-repo deploy, Supabase eliminates need for separate vector DB
evaluation_criteria_coverage.clarity_of_explanation_15pct: Beautiful UI, live agent trace makes AI visible, LangSmith for non-technical founder
evaluation_criteria_coverage.thinking_beyond_15pct: Performance Reporting solved as bonus, Brief-to-Script pipeline outlined as next phase
